flashcards.back: A digital circuit whose outputs depend only on the current combination of input values. It has no internal memory or feedback loops.

flashcards.front: What is a multiplexer (MUX) and what is its primary function?
flashcards.back: A combinational circuit that selects one of many input lines and forwards it to a single output line. The selection is controlled by a set of select lines.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of a decoder?
flashcards.back: A decoder is a combinational circuit that converts binary information from n input lines to a maximum of 2^n unique output lines. It identifies a specific code from its inputs.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a half-adder and a full-adder?
flashcards.back: A half-adder adds two single binary digits and produces a sum and carry output. A full-adder adds three single binary digits (two inputs and a carry-in) and produces a sum and carry-out.

flashcards.front: What is meant by a 'hazard' in a combinational circuit?
flashcards.back: A hazard is a momentary unwanted switching transient at the output, caused by unequal propagation delays along different paths in the circuit.
